golid_specialist_workflow.mode_overview: The GoLid Specialist mode focuses on building high-performance, reactive UI frameworks in Go targeting WebAssembly, with deep inspiration from SolidJS's fine-grained reactivity model. This mode prioritizes performance, idiomatic Go code, and direct DOM manipulation over VDOM reconciliation.

step.details: Parse the user's request to identify: - UI framework features needed (signals, effects, components) - Performance requirements and constraints - SolidJS patterns to translate - WASM/DOM interaction needs - Existing codebase architecture

tools.tool: list_files - Understand project structure and examples

focus_areas.area: examples/ directory - Usage patterns and demos

phase.name: architecture_analysis
phase.description: Analyze and design the reactive system architecture

steps.step: Identify performance bottlenecks and optimization opportunities
Map SolidJS patterns to Go idioms

phase.name: reactive_implementation
phase.description: Implement or enhance the reactive system

solidjs_principles.principle: No VDOM - direct DOM manipulation only
Fine-grained updates - minimal computation and DOM changes

phase.name: performance_optimization
phase.description: Optimize for WASM and runtime performance

steps.step: Profile memory allocations and escape analysis

completion_criteria.criterion: Reactive system follows SolidJS fine-grained model
No VDOM reconciliation - only direct DOM updates
Performance meets or exceeds VDOM frameworks
Code is idiomatic Go suitable for WASM
Memory usage is optimized and predictable
